title: Identification of the Subcompartment-Specific Mitochondrial Proteome by APEX2 Proximity Labeling in Saccharomyces cerevisiae
author: Lorenz Spänle
license_note: PMC full-text XML — Bio-protocol — CC-BY confirmed in article XML
materials: MS-grade H 2 O (VWR Chemicals, catalog number: 23595.
SGal medium lacking biotin (SGal-biotin medium) (see Recipes)

steps.title: Saccharomyces cerevisiae W303 strain ( MATα...
steps.instruction: Saccharomyces cerevisiae W303 strain ( MATα leu2-3,112 trp1-1 can1-100 ura3-1 ade2-1 his3-11,15 [phi+]) (Ralser et al. [17])

steps.title: Saccharomyces cerevisiae W303 strain + Su9-APEX2-FLAG...
steps.instruction: Saccharomyces cerevisiae W303 strain + Su9-APEX2-FLAG ( YPRC∆15-pRNR1- Mia40(1-70)-APEX2-FLAG-tTDH1-HygromycinR ) (see Appendix in [15])

steps.title: Saccharomyces cerevisiae W303 strain + IMS-APEX2-FLAG...
steps.instruction: Saccharomyces cerevisiae W303 strain + IMS-APEX2-FLAG ( YPRCD15-pRNR1-Su9-APEX2-FLAG-tTDH1-HygromycinR ) (see Appendix in [15]) Note: Yeast strains and plasmids used for integration are available upon request. Reagents

steps.title: Drop-out-mix, 20× (DoM) Reagent Final concentration...
steps.instruction: Drop-out-mix, 20× (DoM) Reagent Final concentration Quantity or volume Adenine hemisulfate salt 2.2 mM 400 mg L-Arginine 2.3 mM 400 mg L-Histidine HCL monohydrate 1.9 mM 400 mg L-Isoleucine 4.6 mM 600 mg L-Leucin 15.3 mM 2,000 mg L-Lysin HCl 3.3 mM 600 mg L-Methionine 2.7 mM 400 mg L-Phenylalanine 6.1 mM 1,000 mg L-Threonine 33.3 mM 4,000 mg L-Tryptophan 2.0 mM 400 mg L-Tyrosine 2.2 mM 400 mg Uracil 3.6 mM 400 mg L-Valine 25.6 mM 3,000 mg L-Proline 34.8 mM 4,000 mg Double-distilled water (ddH 2 O) n/a 1 L Total n/a 1 L Autoclave at 120 °C for 20 min.

steps.title: Galactose, 30% Dissolve 30 g of...
steps.instruction: Galactose, 30% Dissolve 30 g of galactose in up to 100 mL of ddH 2 O by stirring and heating up. Autoclave at 120 °C for 20 min.

steps.title: S, 5× Reagent Final concentration Quantity...
steps.instruction: S, 5× Reagent Final concentration Quantity or volume Yeast-nitrogen-base 5× 9.5 g Ammonium sulfate 189.2 mM 25 g ddH 2 O n/a 1 L Total n/a 1 L Autoclave at 120 °C for 20 min.

steps.title: S-biotin, 5× Reagent Final concentration Quantity...
steps.instruction: S-biotin, 5× Reagent Final concentration Quantity or volume Yeast-nitrogen-base w/o biotin 1× 9.5 g Ammonium sulfate 189.2 mM 25 g ddH 2 O n/a 1 L Total n/a 1 L Autoclave at 120 °C for 20 min.

steps.title: SGal medium Reagent Final concentration Quantity...
steps.instruction: SGal medium Reagent Final concentration Quantity or volume 5× S 1× 200 mL 20× DoM 1× 50 mL 30% galactose 2% 67 mL ddH 2 O (autoclaved) n/a 683 mL Total n/a 1 L Mix reagents in an autoclaved bottle.

steps.title: SGal-biotin medium Reagent Final concentration Quantity...
steps.instruction: SGal-biotin medium Reagent Final concentration Quantity or volume 5× S-biotin 1× 200 mL 20× DoM 1× 50 mL 30% galactose 2% 67 mL ddH 2 O (autoclaved) n/a 883 mL Total n/a 1 L Autoclave at 120 °C for 20 min.

steps.title: MP1 buffer Reagent Final concentration Quantity...
steps.instruction: MP1 buffer Reagent Final concentration Quantity or volume Tris 100 mM 12.11 g Dithiothreitol 10 mM 154.25 mg ddH 2 O n/a 100 mL Total n/a 1 L

steps.title: Sorbitol, 2
steps.instruction: Sorbitol, 2.4 M Dissolve 43.72 g of sorbitol in ddH 2 O to a final volume of 100 mL. Autoclave at 120 °C for 20 min.

steps.title: Sorbitol, 1
steps.instruction: Sorbitol, 1.2 M Dissolve 21.86 g of sorbitol in ddH 2 O to a final volume of 100 mL. Autoclave at 120 °C for 20 min.

steps.title: KH 2 PO 4 , 1 M Dissolve 13
steps.instruction: KH 2 PO 4 , 1 M Dissolve 13.61 g of KH 2 PO 4 in ddH 2 O to a final volume of 100 mL.

steps.title: K 2 HPO 4 , 1 M Dissolve 171
steps.instruction: K 2 HPO 4 , 1 M Dissolve 171.8 g of K 2 HPO 4 in ddH 2 O to a final volume of 1 L.

steps.title: KPi buffer, 1 M, pH 7
steps.instruction: KPi buffer, 1 M, pH 7.4 Mix 50 mL of 1 M KH 2 PO 4 with ~525 mL of 1 M K 2 HPO 4 to adjust to pH 7.

steps.title: MP2 buffer Reagent Final concentration Quantity or volume 2
steps.instruction: MP2 buffer Reagent Final concentration Quantity or volume 2.4 M sorbitol 1.2 M 50 mL 1 M KPi buffer pH 7.4 20 mM 2 mL ddH 2 O n/a 48 mL Total n/a 100 mL

steps.title: Tris, 1 M, pH 7,4 Dissolve 121
steps.instruction: Tris, 1 M, pH 7,4 Dissolve 121.1 g of Tris(hydroxymethyl)aminomethane in ~800 mL of ddH 2 O and adjust the pH to 7.4 by adding 37% HCl dropwise. Fill up to 1 L with ddH 2 O.

steps.title: Tris, 20 mM, pH 7
steps.instruction: Tris, 20 mM, pH 7.4 Mix 20 mL of 1 M Tris pH 7.4 with 980 mL of ddH 2 O.

steps.title: EDTA, 0
steps.instruction: EDTA, 0.5 M, pH 8.0 Dissolve 18.6 g of EDTA disodium salt 2-hydrate in ddH 2 O to a final volume of 100 mL. Adjust pH to 8.0 with NaOH.

steps.title: Homogenization buffer (HB) Reagent Final concentration...
steps.instruction: Homogenization buffer (HB) Reagent Final concentration Quantity or volume 1 M Tris pH 7.4 10 mM 2 mL 0.5 M EDTA pH 8.0 1 mM 0.4 mL BSA 0.2% 400 mg PMSF 1 mM 34.84 mg 2.4 M sorbitol 0.6 M 50 mL ddH 2 O n/a 147.6 mL Total n/a 200 mL Critical: Add PMSF and BSA fresh and just before use.

steps.title: HEPES, 1 M, pH 7
steps.instruction: HEPES, 1 M, pH 7.4 Dissolve 23.8 g of HEPES in ddH 2 O to a final volume of 100 mL. Adjust to pH 7.4 with NaOH.

steps.title: SH buffer Reagent Final concentration Quantity or volume 2
steps.instruction: SH buffer Reagent Final concentration Quantity or volume 2.4 M sorbitol 0.6 M 25 mL 1 M HEPES pH 7.4 20 mM 2 mL ddH 2 O n/a 73 mL Total n/a 100 mL

steps.title: Biotin-phenol, 5
steps.instruction: Biotin-phenol, 5.5 mM Dissolve 2 mg of biotin-phenol in DMSO to a final volume of 1 mL.

steps.title: H 2 O 2 , 100...
steps.instruction: H 2 O 2 , 100 mM Dilute 5 μL of 30% H 2 O 2 in 495 μL of ddH 2 O.

steps.title: Ascorbic acid, 250 mM Dissolve 44...
steps.instruction: Ascorbic acid, 250 mM Dissolve 44 mg of ascorbic acid in ddH 2 O to a final volume of 1 mL.

steps.title: Sodium azide, 1% Dissolve 100 mg...
steps.instruction: Sodium azide, 1% Dissolve 100 mg of sodium azide in 9.9 mL of ddH 2 O.

steps.title: Trolox, 100 mM Dissolve 25 mg...
steps.instruction: Trolox, 100 mM Dissolve 25 mg of Trolox in ddH 2 O to a final volume of 1 mL.

steps.title: Quencher cocktail Reagent Final concentration Quantity...
steps.instruction: Quencher cocktail Reagent Final concentration Quantity or volume 250 mM ascorbic acid 10 mM 400 μL 1% sodium azide 10 mM 660 μL 100 mM Trolox 5 mM 500 μL SH buffer n/a 8.44 mL Total n/a 10 mL

steps.title: NaPi buffer, 200 mM, pH 7
steps.instruction: NaPi buffer, 200 mM, pH 7.4 Dissolve 0.47 g of NaH 2 PO 4 ·H 2 O and 2.355 g of Na 2 HPO 4 in ddH 2 O to a final volume of 100 mL.

steps.title: NaCl, 5 M Dissolve 58
steps.instruction: NaCl, 5 M Dissolve 58.44 g of NaCl in ddH 2 O to a final volume of 200 mL.

steps.title: SDS, 10% Dissolve 10 g of...
steps.instruction: SDS, 10% Dissolve 10 g of SDS in up to 100 mL of ddH 2 O.

steps.title: Protease inhibitor, 10× Dissolve 1 tablet...
steps.instruction: Protease inhibitor, 10× Dissolve 1 tablet of protease inhibitor in 1 mL of ddH 2 O.

steps.title: RIPA Reagent Final concentration Quantity or...
steps.instruction: RIPA Reagent Final concentration Quantity or volume 200 mM NaPi buffer pH 7.4 20 mM 10 mL 5 M NaCl 150 mM 3 mL IGEPAL CA-630 1% 1 mL Deoxycholic acid sodium salt 0.5% (w/v) 500 mg 10% SDS 0.1% 1 mL 10× protease inhibitor 1× 10 mL ddH 2 O n/a 75 mL Total n/a 100 mL

steps.title: Urea, 8 M Dilute 96
steps.instruction: Urea, 8 M Dilute 96.1 g of urea in MS-grade ddH 2 O to a final volume of 200 mL.

steps.title: Trypsin, 0
steps.instruction: Trypsin, 0.5 μg/μL Dissolve 20 μg of trypsin (1 ampule) in 40 μL of trypsin resuspension buffer. To activate the trypsin, incubate at 30 °C for 15 min. Leftover trypsin solution can be stored at -80 °C.

steps.title: DTT, 1 M Dissolve 154 mg...
steps.instruction: DTT, 1 M Dissolve 154 mg of DTT in MS-grade ddH 2 O to a final volume of 1 mL

steps.title: Elution buffer I Reagent Final concentration...
steps.instruction: Elution buffer I Reagent Final concentration Quantity or volume 8 M urea 2 M 250 μL 1 M Tris pH 7.4 50 mM 50 μL 1 M DTT 1 mM 1 μL 0.5 μg/μL trypsin 5 ng/μL 10 μL MS-grade ddH 2 O n/a 689 μL Total n/a 1 mL

steps.title: CAA, 400 mM Dissolve 37
steps.instruction: CAA, 400 mM Dissolve 37.4 mg of CAA in MS-grade ddH 2 O to a final volume of 1 mL.

steps.title: Elution buffer II Reagent Final concentration...
steps.instruction: Elution buffer II Reagent Final concentration Quantity or volume 8 M urea 2 M 250 μL 1 M Tris pH 7.4 50 mM 50 μL 400 mM CAA 5 mM 25 μL 0.5 μg/μL trypsin 5 ng/μL 10 μL MS-grade ddH 2 O n/a 665 μL Total n/a 1 mL

steps.title: TFA, 10% Dissolve 5 mL of...
steps.instruction: TFA, 10% Dissolve 5 mL of TFA in 45 mL of MS-grade ddH 2 O.

steps.title: Buffer A Dissolve 50 μL of...
steps.instruction: Buffer A Dissolve 50 μL of formic acid in 50 mL of MS-grade ddH 2 O.

steps.title: Buffer B Mix 50 μL of...
steps.instruction: Buffer B Mix 50 μL of formic acid, 40 mL of acetonitrile, and 10 mL of MS-grade ddH 2 O.

steps.title: Buffer C Mix 100 μL of...
steps.instruction: Buffer C Mix 100 μL of 10% TFA, 10 μL of formic acid, and 10 mL of MS-grade ddH 2 O. Laboratory supplies

steps.title: Pipette tips (10, 200, 1,000 μL)
steps.instruction: Pipette tips (10, 200, 1,000 μL)

steps.title: Inoculate a fresh colony of S
steps.instruction: Inoculate a fresh colony of S. cerevisiae in 20 mL of liquid SGal medium and incubate overnight at 130 rpm and 30 °C. Note: The experiment is performed with biological replicates. Use four different starting colonies for each strain and condition.

steps.title: Dilute the overnight culture in 200...
steps.instruction: Dilute the overnight culture in 200 mL of liquid SGal medium to an optical density of 0.2 at 600 nm (OD 600 ) using the Ultrospec TM cell density meter. Incubate the culture for 8 h at 130 rpm and 30°C.

steps.title: Dilute the culture in 2 L...
steps.instruction: Dilute the culture in 2 L of liquid SGal-biotin medium to an OD 600 of 0.025 and incubate overnight (18 h) at 130 rpm and 30 °C. Note: These dilutions are based on the growth behavior of the cells. Adapt the dilutions to maintain exponential growth before starting the mitochondria isolation in the morning, as they may vary. B. Mitochondria isolation Note: A visual representation of the procedure can be found in [20].

steps.title: Distribute the culture in six 500...
steps.instruction: Distribute the culture in six 500 mL centrifugation bottles to harvest cells in the exponential phase with a Beckman Coulter Avanti J-26 XP (rotor: JA

steps.title: Measure the weight of an empty bottle (W1)
steps.instruction: Measure the weight of an empty bottle (W1).

steps.title: Discard the supernatant and wash the...
steps.instruction: Discard the supernatant and wash the cell pellets with ~15 mL of ddH 2 O. Transfer the resuspended pellets to the previously weighed bottle and spin down at 2,800× g for 5 min.

steps.title: Remove the supernatant and determine the...
steps.instruction: Remove the supernatant and determine the wet weight (WW) of the cell pellet by weighing the bottle again (W2 - W1 = WW).

steps.title: Resuspend the pellet in MP1 buffer...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the pellet in MP1 buffer (2 mL per g WW) and incubate for 10 min at 130 rpm and 30 °C.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 2,800× g for 5 min and remove supernatant
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 2,800× g for 5 min and remove supernatant.

steps.title: Wash the pellet in 50 mL of 1
steps.instruction: Wash the pellet in 50 mL of 1.2 M sorbitol, centrifuge at 2,800× g for 5 min, and remove the supernatant.

steps.title: Dissolve 3 mg/g WW zymolyase in the MP2 buffer (6
steps.instruction: Dissolve 3 mg/g WW zymolyase in the MP2 buffer (6.7 mL/g WW) and resuspend the cell pellet with the prepared MP2 buffer. Incubate the cell solution for 60 min at 130 rpm and 30 °C. Critical: Cool down the centrifuge to 4 °C and work only on ice from now on.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 2,200× g for 5 min and remove supernatant
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 2,200× g for 5 min and remove supernatant.

steps.title: Dissolve and resuspend the pellet in...
steps.instruction: Dissolve and resuspend the pellet in half of the volume of HB (13.4 mL/g WW) and transfer to a cooled Dounce homogenizer.

steps.title: Place the Dounce homogenizer on ice...
steps.instruction: Place the Dounce homogenizer on ice and homogenize the cell solution by gently moving the plunger up and down 15 times.

steps.title: Pour the homogenate back into the...
steps.instruction: Pour the homogenate back into the bottle and wash the homogenizer with the leftover HB. Critical: The next step is to collect the supernatant and not discard it!

steps.title: Centrifuge at 2,800× g for 5...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 2,800× g for 5 min at 4 °C to spin down cell debris. Transfer the supernatant to a fresh bottle. Discard the pellet.

steps.title: Repeat the previous step for as...
steps.instruction: Repeat the previous step for as long as there is a visible pellet.

steps.title: Centrifuge the supernatant at 17,500× g...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the supernatant at 17,500× g for 12 min and resuspend the pellet (mitochondria) in 5 mL of cold SH buffer. Use cut tips or a plastic pipette with bigger openings (see Figure 3 ). Figure

steps.title: Image of the mitochondrial pellet during mitochondrial isolation
steps.instruction: Image of the mitochondrial pellet during mitochondrial isolation. Visible is a small mitochondrial pellet at the bottom of a 500 mL Beckmann centrifugation bottle. The pellet was obtained after centrifugation at 17,500× g for 12 min. The picture was taken before washing with SH buffer. The size of the mitochondrial pellet is the result of a 2 L starting culture.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 17,500× g for 12 min and remove supernatant
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 17,500× g for 12 min and remove supernatant.

steps.title: Resuspend the pellet in 300 μL of SH buffer
steps.instruction: Resuspend the pellet in 300 μL of SH buffer.

steps.title: Take an aliquot to determine protein...
steps.instruction: Take an aliquot to determine protein concentration with a protein assay dye and a UV/Vis spectrometer (Bradford assay).

steps.title: Dilute the mitochondrial suspension to a...
steps.instruction: Dilute the mitochondrial suspension to a final concentration of 10 mg/mL with SH buffer.

steps.title: Snap-freeze 50 μL aliquots in liquid...
steps.instruction: Snap-freeze 50 μL aliquots in liquid nitrogen and store at -80 °C. Pause point: Mitochondrial aliquots can be stored at -80 °C for more than one year. C. Proximity labeling

steps.title: Thaw 50 μL of mitochondrial suspension (10 mg/mL) on ice
steps.instruction: Thaw 50 μL of mitochondrial suspension (10 mg/mL) on ice.

steps.title: Add 38
steps.instruction: Add 38.2 μL of SH buffer and 1.8 μL of 5.5 mM biotin-phenol and incubate mitochondria for 30 min at 900 rpm and 30 °C.

steps.title: Add 1 μL of 30% H...
steps.instruction: Add 1 μL of 30% H 2 O 2 , vortex at low speed, and incubate for 5 min at 30 °C.

steps.title: Add 100 μL of ice-cold quencher...
steps.instruction: Add 100 μL of ice-cold quencher cocktail and centrifuge at 25,000× g for 10 min at 4 °C. Remove the supernatant.

steps.title: Wash with 100 μL of quencher...
steps.instruction: Wash with 100 μL of quencher cocktail and centrifuge at 25,000× g for 10 min at 4 °C. Remove the supernatant.

steps.title: Resuspend the pellet in 500 μL...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the pellet in 500 μL of ice-cold lysis buffer (RIPA).

steps.title: Use the cell disruptor for 15...
steps.instruction: Use the cell disruptor for 15 min at 4 °C to break up the mitochondrial membrane.

steps.title: Centrifuge the lysate at 17,000× g...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the lysate at 17,000× g for 10 min at 4 °C. Transfer the supernatant into a fresh tube. D. Enrichment

steps.title: Wash 40 μL of streptavidin beads...
steps.instruction: Wash 40 μL of streptavidin beads 2× with 500 μL of RIPA buffer. Remove the supernatant with a magnetic rack.

steps.title: Resuspend washed beads in 40 μL...
steps.instruction: Resuspend washed beads in 40 μL of RIPA buffer and add the mitochondrial lysate.

steps.title: Incubate for 1 h tumbling at 4 °C
steps.instruction: Incubate for 1 h tumbling at 4 °C.

steps.title: Separate the beads on a magnetic...
steps.instruction: Separate the beads on a magnetic rack and discard the supernatant.

steps.title: Wash the beads 2× with 250...
steps.instruction: Wash the beads 2× with 250 μL of RIPA buffer and 1× with 250 μL of 20 mM Tris buffer.

steps.title: Again, add 250 μL of 20...
steps.instruction: Again, add 250 μL of 20 mM Tris buffer and transfer everything to a fresh tube to remove excess detergent.

steps.title: Add 50 μL of elution buffer...
steps.instruction: Add 50 μL of elution buffer I and incubate for 1 h at room temperature (RT) with tumbling.

steps.title: Separate beads on a magnetic rack...
steps.instruction: Separate beads on a magnetic rack and transfer supernatant to a fresh tube.

steps.title: Add 50 μL of elution buffer...
steps.instruction: Add 50 μL of elution buffer II to the beads and incubate for 30 min at RT with tumbling.

steps.title: Separate beads on a magnetic rack,...
steps.instruction: Separate beads on a magnetic rack, transfer supernatant, and combine with the first elution.

steps.title: Continue the digest of the elution...
steps.instruction: Continue the digest of the elution overnight (16 h) in the dark at 37 °C. E. Stage tipping and MS Note: See General note

steps.title: Acidify digested peptides by adding 10...
steps.instruction: Acidify digested peptides by adding 10 μL of 10% TFA and spin down the sample at 13,000× g for 1 min.

steps.title: Use 3× C18 membrane layers to purify peptides
steps.instruction: Use 3× C18 membrane layers to purify peptides. Punch the C18 membranes with a spring-loaded syringe and transfer them to a pipette tip [18].

steps.title: Equilibrate the tips with 100 μL...
steps.instruction: Equilibrate the tips with 100 μL of methanol and wash 2× with 100 μL of buffer A. Always spin down the volume using a stage tipping centrifuge.

steps.title: Load acidified peptides (100 μL) onto the stage tip
steps.instruction: Load acidified peptides (100 μL) onto the stage tip.

steps.title: Wash the peptides on the C18...
steps.instruction: Wash the peptides on the C18 membrane with 100 μL of buffer A. Pause point: The peptide-loaded tips can be stored in the fridge for some weeks.

steps.title: Elute peptides with 40 μL of...
steps.instruction: Elute peptides with 40 μL of buffer B by transferring them to a 200 μL Eppendorf tube at 500× g for 5 min. Note: We used an in-house 3D-printed frame to place the stage tips over the Eppendorf tubes ( Figure 2 ).

steps.title: Evaporate the sample in a vacuum...
steps.instruction: Evaporate the sample in a vacuum centrifuge for 2 h at RT.

steps.title: Add 9 μL of buffer C
steps.instruction: Add 9 μL of buffer C. The sample is ready for LC–MS/MS.

description: Validation of protocol This protocol has been used and validated in the following research article (Figure 1): • Flohr et al. [15]. Dysfunctional mitochondria trap proteins in the intermembrane space. EMBO J . (2025), 44: 4352–4377, DOI: https://doi.org/10.1038/s44318-025-00486-1
